signals.headline: HEADLINE:** New Mexico Supreme Court unanimously upholds Duke Rodriguez's ballot eligibility for June Republican guberna
signals.raw: HEADLINE:** New Mexico Supreme Court unanimously upholds Duke Rodriguez's ballot eligibility for June Republican gubernatorial primary

signals.headline: CONFIDENCE:** HIGH
signals.raw:
CONFIDENCE:** HIGH

The New Mexico Supreme Court unanimously ruled that **Republican gubernatorial candidate Duke Rodriguez can appear on the June primary ballot**[1], upholding a lower court decision. Rodriguez is one of three Republicans in the race alongside Greg Hall and Doug Turner, competing in an open primary since Governor Michelle Luhan Gisham cannot run for a third term[1].

This ruling
